items_fr: 2.  All  documents  of  the Commission  which  are circulated  to  Governments  by  the  Secretary-Gen› eral  shall  also  be  circulated  to  such  organs  of the United Nations as are concerned.  Such organs may furnish  any information  or make  any  suggestions to  the Commission.
items_en: 2. All documents of the Commission which are circulated to Governments by the Secretary-Gen› eral shall also be circulated to such organizations of the United Nations as are concerned. Such organizations may provide any information or make any suggestions to the Commission.

text_fr: Article  26
text_en: Article 26

items_fr: 1. The Commission may consult with any inter› national  or national  organizations,  official  or non› official,  on any subject entrusted to it if it believes that such a  procedure might aid it in  the perform› ance of its functions.

2.  For the purpose of distribution of documents of  the  Commission,  the  Secretary-General,  after consultation with the Commission, shall draw up a list of national and international organizations con› cerned  with  questions  of  international  law. The Secretary-General  shall  endeavour  to  include  on this  list  at least  one  national  organization of each Member of the United Nations.

3.  In  the  application  of  the  provisions  of  this article,  the  Commission  and the  Secretary-General shall  comply  with  the  resolutions  of  the  General Assembly  and  the  other  principal  organs  of  the United  Nations  concerning  relations  with  Franco Spain  and  shall  exclude  both  from  consultations and from  the  list,  organizations  which  have  colla› borated  with  the  nazis  and  fascists.

4.  The advisability of consultation by the Com› mission with inter-governmental organizations whose  task  is  the  codification of international law, such  as  those  of  the  Pan-American  Union,  is recognized.
items_en: 1. The Commission may consult with any inter› national or national organizations, official or non› official, on any subject trusted to it if it believes that such a procedure may help it in the performance›ance of its functions.

2. For the purpose of distribution of documents of the Commission, the Secretary-General, after consultation with the Commission, shall draw up a list of national and international organizations con› recognized with questions of international law. The Secretary-General shall undertake to include on this list at least one national organization of each Member of the United Nations.

3. In the application of the provisions of this article, the Commission and the Secretary-General shall comply with the resolutions of the General Assembly and the other main organizations of the United Nations concerning relations with Franco Spain and shall exclude both from consultations and from the list, organizations which have colla› bounded with the Nazis and fascists.
4. The advisory of consultation by the Com› mission with inter-governmental organizations which task is the codification of international law, such as that of the Pan-American Union, is recognized.

text_fr: 176 (II). Enseignement  du  droit  interna› tional
text_en: 176 (II). Teaching of international law

text_fr: Considerant qu'il faut travailler a atteindre Jes buts que vise la resolution 94 (I) 1 du 11  decembre
text_en: Recognising the need to work towards achieving the goals of Resolution 94 (I) 1 of 11 December

text_fr: of  11  December  1946, which initiated the fulfil› ment of Article  13,  paragraph 1, sub-paragraph a,  of  the Charter, regarding the development of international law and its codification;
text_en: of 11 December 1946, which initialled the fulfil› ment of Article 13, paragraph 1, sub-paragraph a, of the Charter, looking at the development of international law and its codification;

text_fr: Considering that  one  of  the  most  effective means  of  furthering  the  development  of  inter› national law consists in promoting public interest in this subject and using  the media of education and publicity to familiarize  the peoples with the principles  and  rules  that  govern  international relations;
text_en: Considering that one of the most effective means of furthering the development of inter› national law consist in promoting public interest in this subject and using the media of education and publicity to familiarize the people with the principals and rules that govern international relations;

text_fr: Considering that  greater  knowledge  of  and fuller information on the aims, purposes and struc› ture  of  the  United  Nations  constitute  another positive  method of  assisting  the development of international law, of which the United Nations is the main instrument,
text_en: Considering that greater knowledge of and fuller information on theims, purposes and structure› ture of the United Nations constitutes another positive method of assisting the development of international law, of which the United Nations is the main instrument,

text_fr: The General Assembly
text_en: The General Assembly

text_fr: Resolves to  request the Governments of Mem› ber States:
text_en: Resolves to request the Governments of Mem› ber States:

items_fr: 1. To take appropriate measures to extend the teaching of  international law in all its phases, in› cluding its  development and codification,  in the universities and higher educational institutions of each country that are under government control or over which Governments have some influence, or to  initiate  such  teaching where  it  is  not  yet provided;

2.  To promote similar teaching regarding the aims,  purposes,  structure  and  operation  of  the United Nations in conjunction with paragraph 1 above and in accordance with resolution 137 (II) adopted by the General Assembly on 17  Novem› ber  194 7, on  the  teaching  of  the  purposes and principles, the structure and activities of the United Nations in the schools of Member States;

3.  To give to the Secretary-General the fullest possible  co-operation  with  a  view  to  facilitating the preparatory work on the development of inter› national law and its  codification  and to support any  individual  or  private  effort  to  these  ends undertaken in their countries.
items_en: 1. To take appropriate measures to extend the teaching of international law in all its phases, in› defining its development and codification, in the universities and higher educational institutions of each country that are under government control or over which Governments have some influence, or to initiate such teaching where it is not yet provided;

2. To promote similar teaching looking at the images, purposes, structure and operation of the United Nations in conjunction with paragraph 1 above and in accordance with resolution 137 (II) adopted by the General Assembly on 17 November› ber 194 7, on the teaching of the purposes and principles, the structure and activities of the United Nations in the schools of Member States;

3. To give to the Secretary-General the full possible co-operation with a view to facilitating the preparatory work on the development of inter› national law and its codification and to support any individual or private effort to these end undertaken in their countries.

text_fr: 1946  de  l'Assemblce generale qui a  inaugurc la mise en application de l'Article 13, paragraphe 1, alinca a, de la Charte, relatif au developpement progressif  du droit international et a sa codifica› tion;
text_en: 1946 of the General Assembly which inaugurated the implementation of Article 13(1)(a) of the Charter concerning the progressive development of international law and its codification;

text_fr: Considerant que l'une des  fai;ons  les  plus  effi› caces  de  travailler  au  developpement  du  droit international  consiste  a  favoriser  l'interet  du public a son  egard  et  a  employer  les  methodes d'cducation et de propagande tendant a familiari› ser  les  peuples  avec les  principes et les  regles  qui regissent les  relations intemationales;
text_en: Recognising that one of the most effective ways of working towards the development of international law is to promote public interest and to use the methods of cducation and propaganda which tend to familialize peoples with the principles and rules which regulate internal relations;

text_fr: Considerant qu'une connaissancc  plus  appro› fondic des buts, des objectifs et de la structure de !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies,  ainsi  qu'une documentation  plus  riche  sur  ces  memes  sujets, constituent d'autres moyens efficaces de cooperer au developpement du droit international dont cet organisme est le principal instrument,
text_en: Recognizing that a more appropriate knowledge of the aims, objectives and structure of the United Nations, as well as a richer documentation on these same subjects, are other effective means of cooperating in the development of international law, of which this body is the main instrument,

text_fr: L' Assem blee  generate
text_en: The Assem blee generate

text_fr: Decide d'inviter les  Gouverncments  des  Etats Membres:
text_en: Decides to invite the Governments of the Member States:

items_fr: 1.  A  prendre les  mesures propres a intensifier l'enseignement du  droit  international  considere clans toutes les  phases de son developpement et sa codification, clans les  universites et etablissements d'enseignement  superieur  de  chaque  pays  qui dependent  du  Gouvernement  ou  clans  lesquels celui-ci peut exercer son influence, ou a organiser cet enseignement clans  les  cas  ou ii  n'existe pas;

2. A  favoriser  de  meme l'enseignement relatif aux buts, aux objets, a la structure et au fonction› nement  de  !'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies, compte tenu de cc qui est <lit  ci-dessus  au para› graphe  1,  et  conformement  a  la  resolution 13 7 (II)  adoptee par l' Assemblee generale le  1 7 novembre 1947, relative a l'enseignement des buts et des  principes, de la structure et de l'activite de !'Organisation des  Nations Unies clans  les  ecoles des  Etats Membres;

3.  A  preter au Secretaire general tout le  con› cours  en  leur  pouvoir  pour faciliter  les  travaux de preparation a la codification du droit interna› tional et a son developpement, et d'accorder leur appui a toute  initiative  particuliere  OU  privee prise clans leurs pays respcctifs et tendant aux fins susdites.
items_en: 1. To take appropriate measures to intensify the teaching of international law in all phases of its development and codification, including the universities and higher education establishments of each country which depends on or has no influence on the Government or clans, or to organize such teaching in cases where it does not exist;

2. To promote even the teaching of the purposes, objects, structure and function of the United Nations, taking into account the provisions of paragraph 1 above, and in accordance with resolution 13 7 (II) adopted by the General Assembly on 1 7 November 1947 on the teaching of the purposes and principles, structure and activity of the United Nations in the schools of the Member States;

3. To provide the Secretary-General with all the assistance in their power to facilitate the work of preparing for the codification of international law and its development, and to grant their support to any particular initiative or depriving them of their respective countries and for the purposes set forth above.
